title: Digital Guardian Demo Playbook
content:
This playbook will show how to handle an exfiltration event through Digital Guardian by emailing a user's manager and adding the user to a DG Watchlist.

Cortex XSOAR/XSIAM SOAR playbook from the Digital Guardian pack (DigitalGuardian, partner-supported).

Inputs — User Name: User Name to check; Watchlist Name: The name of the DG watchlist to add the user to.; Incident Match: The incident name should contain this string in order for the playbook to handle the event.  The default is DLP1008 which is a USB Exfiltration event.; Notify Manager: Notify User's Manager.

Integrations/scripts used: Digital Guardian, send-mail.
raw_yaml:

version: -1
name: Digital Guardian Demo Playbook
description: This playbook will show how to handle an exfiltration event through Digital
  Guardian by emailing a user's manager and adding the user to a DG Watchlist.
starttaskid: '0'
tasks:

      templateParams: {}
      to:

          root: UserManagerEmail
      transientFile: {}
      transientFileCID: {}
      transientFileContent: {}

      exitCondition: ''

      'yes':
      - '2'
    separatecontext: false

    separatecontext: false

    note: false
    timertriggers: []
    ignoreworker: false
view: "{\n  \"linkLabelsPosition\": {\n    \"20_2_yes\": 0.41\n  },\n  \"paper\":\
  \ {\n    \"dimensions\": {\n      \"height\": 1785,\n      \"width\": 2120,\n  \
  \    \"x\": 50,\n      \"y\": 50\n    }\n  }\n}"
inputs:
- key: User Name
  value:
    simple: ${incident.digitalguardianusername}
  required: true
  description: User Name to check
- key: Watchlist Name
  value:
    simple: DLP-USB-USERS
  required: true
  description: The name of the DG watchlist to add the user to.
- key: Incident Match
  value:
    simple: DLP1008
  required: true
  description: The incident name should contain this string in order for the playbook
    to handle the event.  The default is DLP1008 which is a USB Exfiltration event.
- key: Notify Manager
  value:
    simple: 'true'
  required: false
  description: Notify User's Manager
outputs: []
